summary: Closed, unmerged PR that attempted to advance menu main-rule decompilation by implementing several previously stubbed mnmainrule functions, tightening related mnmain and mnmainrule prototypes, and using dont_inline pragmas to keep small numeric-formatting helpers from being inlined. The main implemented bodies covered text/SIS cleanup and recreation in mn_802307F8 plus menu-flow setup in mn_8023164C and mn_80231714. Evidence is limited to the diff slice; no review discussion or PR body text was available.

key_files.reason: Primary implementation file; added externs, a local GObj bitfield layout, and C bodies for mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, and mn_80231714.
Updated prototypes for newly implemented mnmainrule functions from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures.
Moved existing decompiled mn_8022EA08, mn_8022EA78, mn_8022EB24, and mn_8022EB78 out of the nonmatching-stub region and wrapped two helpers with dont_inline pragmas.
Cleaned several menu-main function declarations to concrete void signatures.
Diff excerpt shows a new build validation marker, suggesting the branch had been locally build-validated.

what_changed: Implemented mn_802307F8(s32 arg0, s32 arg1, s32 arg2), replacing a placeholder marker in mnmainrule.c.

mn_802307F8 treats arg0 as a byte-addressed data pointer, frees or clears an existing SIS/text object at offset 0x130 via HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4, selects a text/index byte from mn_804D4B96 or mn_803EC818 based on arg1 and arg2, creates a new HSD_Text via HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC, stores it back at offset 0x130, sets font_size.x and font_size.y from mn_804DBE2C, and calls HSD_SisLib_803A6368.

Implemented mn_8023164C(void), setting mn_804A04F0.prev_menu from cur_menu, forcing cur_menu to 0xD, choosing hovered_selection and mode based on previous menu 0x0F or 0x10, creating a GObj/proc with fn_8022F538, assigning render_plink, and dispatching mn_80230E38(mode) through HSD_GObj_80390CD4.
Implemented mn_80231714(void) with the same menu-flow and GObj setup as mn_8023164C, then additionally sets mn_804D6BD4 = 1.
Added a local GObjBitfield typedef with padding and bitfields so the implementation could assign the render_plink field on an HSD_GObj-shaped object.
Added extern declarations in mnmainrule.c for mn_804D6BD4, mn_804D4B96, mn_803EC818, and several float constants mn_804DBE18 through mn_804DBE2C.
Changed mnmainrule.h prototypes for mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, and mn_80231714 from unknown signatures to concrete void-returning signatures.
Changed mnmain.h prototypes for mn_8022EBDC, mn_8022F268, mn_8022F360, and mn_8022F4CC from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void-returning signatures where applicable.
Added #pragma dont_inline on/off around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78 in mnmain.c.

Removed placeholder comments for mn_8022EA08, mn_8022EA78, mn_8022EB24, and mn_8022EB78 from the later unresolved-function section of mnmain.c, reflecting that those functions had concrete definitions earlier in the file.
smart_moves: Used #pragma dont_inline around small helper functions mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78, a common matching tactic when the compiler would otherwise inline simple wrappers or formatting helpers and disturb function boundaries.
Tightened headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures as functions became understood, improving type safety and making future callsite work easier.
Kept mn_8023164C and mn_80231714 structurally parallel, making the single behavioral difference, mn_804D6BD4 = 1 in mn_80231714, easy to audit.
Modeled the GObj render_plink assignment with a minimal local bitfield overlay instead of requiring a broad HSD_GObj struct rewrite in this PR.
Preserved unknown data-layout access in mn_802307F8 with explicit offsets such as 0x130 when the surrounding struct was not yet identified, allowing decomp progress without inventing a full struct.
decomp_lessons: When only one or two object fields are known, a small local overlay or byte-pointer offset access can be an acceptable temporary bridge, but it should remain searchable for later struct recovery.
For menu code, global MenuFlow state updates often need exact ordering: save previous menu, set current menu, then derive hovered_selection and mode from the previous value.

Functions that appear nearly identical should be kept textually similar during matching; isolate the actual behavioral delta rather than refactoring into shared helpers that the original binary likely did not have.
SIS/text object lifecycle code should clear stale pointers after cleanup before creating a replacement object, as shown by the HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4 call followed by writing NULL to offset 0x130.
As symbols become decompiled, remove stale placeholder markers and update declarations in headers so later decompilation can rely on real types instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Small numeric/string helpers may require dont_inline pragmas to preserve separate functions and match the target binary.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, and fn_8022F538.
Global data used address-based mn_804Dxxxx and mn_803Exxxx names when semantic names were not yet known.
Known asset or scene descriptors kept descriptive names such as MenMain_fog, MenMain_lights, MenMain_cam, MenMainCursorIs_Top, MenMainConSs_Top, and MenMainCursorSs_Top.
Temporary locals in decomp work used generic register-derived or role-derived names such as data, prev, gobj, mode, r30, sis, and zero.
The local overlay type was named GObjBitfield to describe the matching need around HSD_GObj bitfield access rather than claiming a final engine type.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied #pragma dont_inline on/off around mn_8022EA08 and mn_8022EA78 to prevent unwanted inlining.
Used explicit byte-pointer casts and fixed offsets, especially data + 0x130 and data + 2, to reproduce known object-layout accesses without a completed struct.
Used a bitfield struct with u8 pad[0xD], a 2-bit unknown field, a 2-bit render_plink field, and a 4-bit unknown field to match packed GObj flag assignment.
Preserved switch cases for previous menu values 0x0F and 0x10 plus a default path, likely reflecting the original branch structure.
Used `(u32)(arg1 - 5) <= 1` for the range check on arg1 values 5 and 6, a typical decomp expression chosen to match unsigned comparison codegen.
Introduced local scoped blocks around HSD_Text creation and font-size assignment in mn_802307F8, possibly to influence stack/local lifetime or code shape.

review_feedback.text: The PR context reports one issue comment but provides no body excerpt, no review comments, and no review examples, so no concrete reviewer feedback can be extracted.
The PR was closed without being merged; the slice does not explain whether this was due to matching problems, supersession, style issues, or another reason.

follow_up_queries: Find the issue comment on PR #2081 to determine why it was closed unmerged.
Check whether later PRs implemented or renamed mn_802307F8, mn_8023164C, or mn_80231714 with improved struct typing.
Search for later replacements of the local GObjBitfield overlay with a proper HSD_GObj field definition.
Verify whether mn_804D4B96, mn_803EC818, and mn_804DBE18 through mn_804DBE2C later received semantic names.
Compare later mnmain.h and mnmainrule.h declarations to see which prototype cleanups from this PR were retained.
